frameworks.name: distributed competitive decision-making
frameworks.one_line: competition between choice-selective neural populations across cortical areas shapes choice and confidence

frameworks.name: intra-cortical winner-take-all competition
frameworks.one_line: premotor competing signals each contribute uniquely to confidence rather than only the winner

frameworks.name: consensus / evidence accumulation across modules
frameworks.one_line: confidence reflects agreement among many local decision modules across cortex

core_question: Does subjective confidence reflect the consensus among choice-selective signals distributed across many cortical areas?
key_assumptions: choice-selective neural populations exist across many cortical regions
MEG choice-selective signals index distributed decision dynamics
confidence is a graded, reportable signal tied to decision computations
manipulations: difficult perceptual decisions in human participants
tracking signals favoring chosen vs alternative option per trial
varying which cortical area supports the selected option
key_findings: premotor cortex showed competing choice-selective signals for chosen and alternative options
both competing premotor signals made opposing and unique contributions to confidence
number and strength of choice-selective signals in associative cortex supporting the choice added to confidence
interpretations: confidence emerges from cortex-wide competitive dynamics monitoring consensus across local decision modules
